sites.what: Primary molecule pour / materialization. When graph-apply is enabled and beads.GraphApplyFor(store) resolves an applier, builds a GraphApplyPlan (buildRecipeApplyPlan) and applies the whole formula-v2 topology (root molecule + step/wisp beads + edges) via instantiateViaGraphApply -> applier.ApplyGraphPlan. The recipe root is coerced to type=molecule (line 169); step beads keep their action types for graph.v2 workflows.
sites.currentRouting: Via the `store beads.Store` ARGUMENT, not a Router assertion. beads.GraphApplyFor(store) (graph_apply.go:24) walks GraphApplyHandleProvider; today `store` is policy(Router(work+graph)) so it resolves to the Router's routedGraphApplier (router.go:134/150), which classifies the plan via coordclass.ClassifyGraphPlan and applies it on the ClassGraph backend (the gcg SQLite at .gc/beads.sqlite, or gcg Postgres). No caller in this file references coordrouter.
sites.classAwareReplacement: No change to molecule.go itself — it is store-agnostic. The fix is at the CALLERS: each must pass a store whose GraphApplyFor resolves to the dedicated graph store. Either (a) keep passing the work/control store but have wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies (beadPolicyGraphStore) source its applier from a resolveGraphStore(workStore,cfg,cityPath,rec) instead of beads.GraphApplyFor(innerWork) (see bead_policy_store.go:48), or (b) resolve the graph store at each caller and pass it. Option (a) is the minimal, single-chokepoint replacement that preserves the legacy .gc/beads.sqlite location.

sites.what: When IsGraphApplyEnabled() is false OR beads.GraphApplyFor(store) returns ok=false, falls back to sequential creation of every graph bead: store.Create per step (663), store.Update for parent/assignee (689/729), store.DepAdd for edges (695/713), activateFencedGraphWorkflowBead (742). This creates type=molecule/step/wisp beads one-by-one.
sites.currentRouting: Same `store` argument. Each store.Create/Update/DepAdd routes through policy(Router): the Router's by-id mutation/Create classifies the bead to ClassGraph and lands it on the graph backend. This is the SECOND graph-create path that depends on Router routing and is easy to miss.
sites.classAwareReplacement: Covered by the same caller-store fix: if the store handed to Instantiate routes graph creates to the graph store (via the beadPolicyGraphStore sourcing graph from resolveGraphStore, or the caller passing the graph store), both the apply path AND this sequential fallback land on the correct store. Note: the fallback uses plain store.Create (not the applier), so a fix that only swaps the GraphApplyStore applier and leaves Create on the work store would orphan beads whenever graph-apply is disabled.

sites.what: Fanout fragment materialization: stamps a rootless recipe fragment onto an existing workflow root. When graph-apply enabled and beads.GraphApplyFor(store) ok, builds a fragment plan (buildFragmentApplyPlan) and applies via instantiateFragmentViaGraphApply -> applier.ApplyGraphPlan. Otherwise (line 792+) sequential store.Create/Update/DepAdd (890/911/917/934/949).
sites.currentRouting: Same `store` argument -> policy(Router). graph-apply path uses routedGraphApplier; sequential path uses store.Create. The fragment plan reads existing logical-bead index from `store` (existingLogicalBeadIDIndex at 348/798) and root priority via store.Get(opts.RootID) — those reads must hit the graph store where the root lives.
sites.classAwareReplacement: Same caller-store fix. CAUTION: buildFragmentApplyPlan does store.Get(RootID) and existingLogicalBeadIDIndex(store,RootID) — for graph.v2 the root is a gcg-* graph bead, so the store passed must be able to Get graph beads. The policy(Router) does this via backendForID; a class-aware replacement store must read graph beads (resolveGraphStore) for these reads too, not just the apply.

sites.what: The actual ApplyGraphPlan invocation for both Instantiate and InstantiateFragment (lines 60 and 114). Validates the result against the plan.
sites.currentRouting: Receives `applier beads.GraphApplyStore` already resolved by the caller via beads.GraphApplyFor(store). Today that is the Router's routedGraphApplier.
sites.classAwareReplacement: No change — operates on the applier handed in. The applier source is the lever (see molecule.go callers / bead_policy_store).

sites.what: THE single graph-apply chokepoint for every store opened via openStoreAtForCity/openControlStoreAtForCity. wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies probes beads.GraphApplyFor(store) on the INNER store and, if present, returns a beadPolicyGraphStore that exposes ApplyGraphPlan, applying the wisp/workflow storage-tier policy then delegating to s.applier (the inner store's applier = today the Router's routedGraphApplier).
sites.currentRouting: s.applier = beads.GraphApplyFor(inner). inner is the Router (when graphRelocated) so s.applier routes plans to the ClassGraph backend. When the Router is deleted, routedPolicyStore returns policy(workBackend) with NO Router, so beads.GraphApplyFor(workBackend) resolves the WORK store's applier -> graph plans land on the work store. THIS IS THE ORPHAN LANDMINE realized.
sites.classAwareReplacement: Make wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies (or a new graph-aware wrapper it builds) source its applier from a NEW resolveGraphStore(workBackend, cfg, cityPath, rec) instead of beads.GraphApplyFor(workBackend). resolveGraphStore must: graph=sqlite -> open at the LEGACY .gc/ location (citylayout.RuntimeRoot) with prefix graphStoreIDPrefix='gcg', retention(0,0), reusing graphStoreHandleCache (NOT openClassSQLiteStore/.gc/graph/); graph=postgres -> openClassPostgresStore(cfg,cityPath,BeadClassGraph,nil); graph=bd -> the work store. This preserves the legacy SQLite path and keeps the chokepoint design (one wrapper, all molecule callers fixed at once).

sites.what: Controller order dispatch: pours the order's wisp recipe via molecule.Instantiate, creating the wisp root (type=molecule/wisp) + steps. Then workStore.Update(rootID,...) stamps the root (1435).
sites.currentRouting: workStore comes from m.storeFn(target) = openStoreAtForCity(target.ScopeRoot, cityPath) (order_dispatch.go:416), which ends in routedPolicyStore -> policy(Router) when graphRelocated. So the wisp graph beads route to the graph backend today via the Router.
sites.classAwareReplacement: Once resolveGraphStore backs the policy wrapper's applier (and the legacy sequential Create path), this caller needs no change — it keeps passing workStore. If instead callers resolve graph explicitly, dispatchWisp would pass resolveGraphStore(workStore,...) to Instantiate. The workStore.Update(rootID) at 1435 also targets the (graph) root, so the store must still Get/Update the gcg root.

sites.what: Sling's formula-v2 instantiation: molecule.Instantiate(ctx, deps.Store, recipe, opts) creates the workflow root + steps. Surrounding code closes/replaces prior graph.v2 roots (closeReplacedGraphV2Root 1282, closeFailedGraphV2Roots 1292) and restores snapshots — all via deps.Store.
sites.currentRouting: deps.Store is set from `store` at the sling entry points: cmd_sling.go:393 (Store: store) where store=openStoreAtForCity (313/505/524), and cmd_convoy_dispatch.go:400 (view.store). Both routedPolicyStore -> policy(Router). So graph.v2 roots route to the graph backend today.
sites.classAwareReplacement: Same chokepoint fix covers the apply. NOTE the replace/rollback helpers (closeReplacedGraphV2Root/closeFailedGraphV2Roots/RestoreWorkflowBeads) read and close gcg-* graph roots through deps.Store — they too must reach the graph store, so the store must route graph by-id ops (Get/Close/Update), not just ApplyGraphPlan. A fix that only graph-routes the applier but leaves by-id ops on work would break these close/rollback paths.

sites.what: CLI `gc formula cook`: molecule.Instantiate creates the graph.v2 workflow root + steps and attaches it to a source bead (ensureFormulaCookAttachDep 723). Conflict pre-checks read live roots via store (formulaCookLiveInputConvoyGraphRoots 691, ListLiveRoots 699).
sites.currentRouting: store = openStoreAtForCity(scope.storeRoot, cityPath) (cmd_formula.go:643) -> routedPolicyStore -> policy(Router). Graph beads route to the graph backend today.
sites.classAwareReplacement: Same chokepoint fix. The live-root conflict reads (ListLiveRoots, formulaCookLiveInputConvoyGraphRoots) query gcg-* roots through store, so the store must List/Get graph beads.

sites.what: CLI single-shot order run: molecule.Instantiate pours the order wisp recipe (root + steps).
sites.currentRouting: store resolved upstream via openStoreAtForCity/resolveStore for the order target (cmd_order.go:1326/1749) -> routedPolicyStore -> policy(Router). Graph beads route via Router today.
sites.classAwareReplacement: Same chokepoint fix; no per-caller change if the policy wrapper sources graph from resolveGraphStore.

sites.what: Control dispatcher DRAIN: for each drain row, molecule.Instantiate creates a graph.v2 item workflow (root + steps) under the drain control. On failure closeFailedDrainItemRoots(store,...) (946). Also drain.go:855 store.Create makes the unit convoy bead (type=convoy) that anchors the items.
sites.currentRouting: store is the ProcessControl store, opened by cmd/gc/cmd_convoy_dispatch.go:155 openControlStoreAtForCity -> controlStoreWithGraphRouting -> routedPolicyStore -> policy(Router) when graphRelocated. So drain item graph beads route to the graph backend; without the Router wrap ProcessControl would read Dolt alone and never see sqlite-resident molecule beads (the E0 mediation noted in controlStoreWithGraphRouting).
sites.classAwareReplacement: Same chokepoint fix, but the control path's wrap is controlStoreWithGraphRouting (cmd_convoy_dispatch.go:457) which calls routedPolicyStore. That helper must be rebuilt to wrap the control bd store with a graph-aware policy store whose applier+by-id graph ops resolve via resolveGraphStore at the legacy .gc/ location. The control store also must READ graph beads (ProcessControl scope-check List/Get over gcg-*), so the replacement must keep graph reads class-aware too, not only the apply.

sites.what: Control dispatcher FANOUT: molecule.InstantiateFragment stamps each fragment's step beads onto the workflow root (rootID), expanding fanout work. Reads existing fragment instances + workflow beads via store (resolveExistingFragmentInstanceFromBeads 161, routeFanoutFragmentSteps 159).
sites.currentRouting: Same ProcessControl store -> openControlStoreAtForCity -> controlStoreWithGraphRouting -> policy(Router). Fragment graph beads route to the graph backend; the existing-instance reads also hit graph via the Router.
sites.classAwareReplacement: Same controlStoreWithGraphRouting rebuild. The fragment resume reads (resolveExistingFragmentInstanceFromBeads, workflowBeads list) query gcg-* beads through store, so the replacement must route graph List/Get/Children class-aware in addition to ApplyGraphPlan.

sites.what: Control dispatcher RALPH/RETRY: creates the next retry attempt's beads. Graph-apply path builds a plan and applies it (appendRalphRetryViaGraphApply, applier.ApplyGraphPlan at 610) cloning prior subject/check graph beads as new attempt nodes + edges. Legacy path (appendRalphRetryLegacy) does store.Create for the new subject (432), attempt members (469), and check (502) plus DepAdd. Resolves existing attempts via resolveExistingRalphRetryFromBeads(store,...) (384).
sites.currentRouting: store = ProcessControl store -> openControlStoreAtForCity -> controlStoreWithGraphRouting -> policy(Router). beads.GraphApplyFor(store) (396) -> routedGraphApplier today; legacy store.Create (432/469/502) -> Router by-id Create classifies to ClassGraph. Both retry-create paths land attempt beads on the graph backend via the Router.
sites.classAwareReplacement: Same controlStoreWithGraphRouting rebuild covers BOTH the apply and the legacy Create paths (they share `store`). The legacy path's store.Create + DepAdd + the existing-attempt reads (resolveExistingRalphRetryFromBeads, appendRalphRetryGraphEdges store.Get at 596/600) must all resolve to the graph store — so the replacement must route graph Create/Update/DepAdd/Get/List, not only ApplyGraphPlan.

notes:
SCOPE: I mapped every PRODUCTION caller that CREATES graph beads or APPLIES a graph plan (beads.GraphApplyFor/ApplyGraphPlan/ApplyGraphPlanWithStorage, plus the sequential store.Create fallbacks that create type=molecule/wisp/step graph beads). The recon JSON covered READ paths; this fills the CREATE/APPLY surface.

KEY ARCHITECTURAL FINDING (matches recon's read-path conclusion): the graph CREATE/APPLY surface is ENTIRELY store-mediated. ZERO callers type-assert *coordrouter.Router. Every caller receives a `store beads.Store` and calls beads.GraphApplyFor(store) (or plain store.Create in the legacy fallback). Graph routing exists today only because routedPolicyStore (cmd/gc/api_state.go:252) and controlStoreWithGraphRouting (cmd/gc/cmd_convoy_dispatch.go:457) INSERT the Router with a ClassGraph backend registered. Delete the Router and those wrappers return policy(workBackend) -> beads.GraphApplyFor + store.Create resolve the WORK backend -> ALL graph beads orphan onto the work store. That is the landmine, realized at exactly two wrapper functions.

THE TWO CHOKEPOINTS TO REBUILD (the entire fix is here; no molecule/dispatch/sling code changes needed):
  1. routedPolicyStore (api_state.go:252) — used by openStoreAtForCity/openStoreResultAtForCity (covers order_dispatch dispatchWisp, sling, cmd_formula cook, cmd_order run).
  2. controlStoreWithGraphRouting (cmd_convoy_dispatch.go:457) — used by openControlStoreAtForCity (covers ProcessControl: drain, fanout, ralph/retry).
Both must produce a store whose graph-class ops (ApplyGraphPlan AND by-id Create/Update/Close/DepAdd/Get AND List/Children/Ready for graph beads) resolve to a dedicated graph store, while work ops stay on the work backend.

resolveGraphStore CONTRACT (new function, the class-aware replacement; must NOT reuse resolveClassStore/openClassSQLiteStore):
  - graph=bd -> return the work store (graphRelocated false; byte-identical default).
  - graph=sqlite -> open at the LEGACY location filepath.Join(cityPath, citylayout.RuntimeRoot) i.e. .gc/ (the .gc/beads.sqlite file), prefix graphStoreIDPrefix='gcg', WithSQLiteStoreRetention(0,0), reusing graphStoreHandleCache and the noCloseSQLiteStore wrapper. CRITICAL: do NOT route through openClassSQLiteStore — that uses classSQLiteDir = .gc/graph/ (cmd_beads_migrate.go:26), a DIFFERENT empty path; pointing live maintainer-city (graph_store=sqlite) there orphans live graph data. This exactly replicates registerGraphStoreSQLite (api_state.go:358).
  - graph=postgres -> openClassPostgresStore(cfg, cityPath, config.BeadClassGraph, nil) — recorder nil (graph stays event-silent), schema/prefix 'gcg'. This IS the canonical class path already (replicates api_state.go:348), so it is safe to share with the class-store opener.

WHY by-id and List graph ops matter, not just ApplyGraphPlan: the sequential fallback in molecule.Instantiate/InstantiateFragment (graph-apply disabled or applier absent) creates graph beads via plain store.Create/Update/DepAdd (molecule.go:663/689/695/890/911/917). The dispatch ralph legacy path (ralph.go:432/469/502) and drain unit-convoy create (drain.go:855) do the same. And the apply paths' surrounding logic reads/closes gcg-* roots (sling closeReplacedGraphV2Root, cmd_formula ListLiveRoots, fanout resolveExistingFragmentInstanceFromBeads, ralph resolveExistingRalphRetryFromBeads) plus ProcessControl scope-check List/Get over graph beads. A replacement that graph-routes ONLY the GraphApplyStore applier and leaves by-id/List on the work store would (a) orphan beads whenever graph-apply is disabled and (b) break every replace/rollback/resume/scope-check read. The Router routes the WHOLE graph class (Get/List/Ready/Create/Update/Close/DepAdd + ApplyGraphPlan); the class-aware graph store must do likewise — i.e. the policy wrapper must delegate ALL graph-class ops to resolveGraphStore (matching how sessions/orders/nudges already split via resolveClassStore), with storeref.PrefixOwner('gcg') deciding graph-vs-work for by-id ops.

  /data/projects/gascity/.claude/worktrees/infra-store-plan/cmd/gc/bead_policy_store.go (beadPolicyGraphStore 33/243, wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies 40)
  /data/projects/gascity/.claude/worktrees/infra-store-plan/cmd/gc/api_state.go (routedPolicyStore 252, registerGraphStoreSQLite 358, graphStoreHandleCache 320, graphStoreIDPrefix 276)
  /data/projects/gascity/.claude/worktrees/infra-store-plan/cmd/gc/class_store.go (resolveClassStore 158, openClassSQLiteStore 95 — the WRONG path for graph)
  /data/projects/gascity/.claude/worktrees/infra-store-plan/cmd/gc/cmd_beads_migrate.go (classSQLiteDir 26 = .gc/<class>/, classSQLitePrefix 22)
  /data/projects/gascity/.claude/worktrees/infra-store-plan/cmd/gc/postgres_class_store.go (openClassPostgresStore 48)
  /data/projects/gascity/.claude/worktrees/infra-store-plan/cmd/gc/cmd_convoy_dispatch.go (openControlStoreAtForCity 422, controlStoreWithGraphRouting 457, ProcessControl call 243)
  /data/projects/gascity/.claude/worktrees/infra-store-plan/cmd/gc/order_dispatch.go (dispatchWisp Instantiate 1410, storeFn 415)
  /data/projects/gascity/.claude/worktrees/infra-store-plan/internal/sling/sling.go:1287

NOTE on coordclass: coordclass.ClassifyGraphPlan (used by routedGraphApplier to pick the backend) has NO production caller outside the Router and dies with it. A resolveGraphStore-based replacement does not need it — the city-level graph store is unconditional for the graph class once selected, and storeref.PrefixOwner('gcg') handles by-id ownership. coordclass.Classify/Classes/ClassWork survive via internal/storemigrate (unaffected)."
